title: Thelypteris sumatrana
taxonomy: Thelypteris sumatrana is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is a member of the genus Thelypteris, which is a group of ferns that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Thelypteris sumatrana is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Indonesia and Malaysia. The classification of Thelypteris sumatrana is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. This fern is known for its unique frond shape and is often used in horticultural settings for its ornamental value.
uses: Thelypteris sumatrana has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and infections. In some cultures, it is also used to treat fever and respiratory issues. Additionally, the plant is believed to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Thelypteris sumatrana for medicinal purposes.

parts: Thelypteris sumatrana is a species of fern known to have medicinal properties.
